data.pattern_philosophy: Quiet, warm background texture drawn from the brand's food vocabulary — bowls, plates, spice dots, a level 'balance' line. Patterns are atmosphere, never noise; they sit at low opacity in negative space and never crowd content or numbers.

patterns.name: Spice-dot field
patterns.description: A sparse, irregular scatter of small dots (spice / cardamom / mustard seeds) in Oat on Cream. The signature ambient texture.
patterns.construction: Dots 3-6px, randomised on a loose 48px grid; ~8-12% coverage; two tones of oat for subtle depth.
patterns.usage: Empty states, splash, email headers, section dividers. Never behind body text or macro/price figures.

patterns.name: Bowl tile
patterns.description: A soft repeating tile of outlined bowls and plates at varied sizes, gently overlapping, all in one tone.
patterns.construction: Line-only bowls, 2px stroke, seamless tile; large scale so individual bowls read; low contrast.
patterns.usage: Marketing section backgrounds, app onboarding cards, the 'your month, planned' band.

patterns.name: Balance line
patterns.description: A single calm horizontal line motif with a soft node at centre — the 'balanced' idea as a divider or footer band.
patterns.construction: 1-2px line, centre node = small bowl or dot; used as a rule, not a fill.
patterns.usage: Section rules, footer, between plan days, email separators.

patterns.name: Macro arc motif
patterns.description: Three nested soft arcs (protein/carb/fat) as a decorative corner or watermark for value/nutrition sections — decorative only, never a real chart.
patterns.construction: Three concentric partial arcs; saffron/green/clove; used large and faint.
patterns.usage: Corner watermark on nutrition/value spots and premium-tier cards.
patterns.note: Decorative motif only — must not be mistaken for an actual macro readout; keep abstract and faint.

do: Keep patterns in negative space and at low opacity.
Use one pattern per surface; don't stack them.
Match pattern tone to the surface (oat on cream, cream on green).
Let cream and calm lead; patterns are a whisper.
dont: Never place a pattern behind body text, macros, prices, or the ledger.
Never use a pattern loud enough to compete with food photography.
Never render the macro-arc motif as if it were real data.
No busy full-bleed maximalist patterns; Rasa is calm.
